food_subgroup: Chinese / Cantonese steamed cakes and dim sum desserts

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Thousand Layer Cake (千层糕). The closest defensible FoodOn anchor is the generic steamed cake food product class. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Steamed cake) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for Cantonese thousand-layer steamed cake in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Cantonese dim sum dessert. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual ethnic confectionery items at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Thousand Layer Cake is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one or two slices as served at a Cantonese dim sum restaurant or tea house.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cake, sponge (SR Legacy ID 18101) and Cake, yellow, commercially prepared (SR Legacy ID 18139), used as composite reference for steamed egg-and-flour layered cake
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Cantonese Thousand Layer Cake (千层糕) exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake and egg-enriched steamed cake composites, cross-referenced with the input enriched_json values (confidence 0.9). The input values are consistent with USDA sponge cake profiles adjusted for the salted egg yolk and condensed milk layers. Saturated fat estimated at 3.2 g per 100 g based on USDA SR Legacy sponge cake fat composition. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown is not available in approved sources for this preparation. Cholesterol estimated at 95 mg per 100 g based on egg and salted egg yolk content per USDA data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat-flour-based steamed cakes and sponge cakes — analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour cake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Cantonese Thousand Layer Cake (千层糕) is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated by analogy with refined wheat flour sponge cake entries (GI range 66–75) in the University of Sydney GI Database. The presence of eggs, condensed milk, and salted egg yolk provides moderate fat and protein that slightly attenuates the glycemic response, but the dominant refined flour and sugar matrix supports a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 42.5) / 100 = 29.75 for a 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 180 mg per 100 g, contributed primarily by salted duck egg yolk layers
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice and avoid versions with heavy salted egg yolk layers, as cumulative sodium intake across a dim sum meal can exceed recommended daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high sugar content (18.5 g per 100 g) from refined wheat flour and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Thousand Layer Cake due to its high GI and significant sugar load, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, a very small portion (30–40 g) paired with a protein-rich food may partially blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.2 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~95 mg per 100 g) from egg yolk and condensed milk, combined with high refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Thousand Layer Cake only occasionally and in small portions, as the combination of saturated fat, dietary cholesterol, and high refined sugar content is unfavourable for lipid management and cardiovascular risk reduction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thousand Layer Cake (千层糕, Qiāncénggāo) is a classic Cantonese dim sum dessert originating from Guangdong Province in southern China. Despite its name, the cake typically comprises 7 to 12 distinct alternating layers rather than a literal thousand. The pale sponge layers are made from a light batter of refined wheat flour, eggs, and sugar, while the darker, richer layers incorporate salted duck egg yolk, sweetened condensed milk, or mashed taro. Each layer is steamed individually and allowed to partially set before the next layer of batter is poured on top, a painstaking process that produces the cake's signature striped cross-section. The finished cake has a soft, bouncy, and airy texture with a delicate sweetness offset by the savory-rich notes of salted egg yolk. It is traditionally served at Cantonese tea houses (yum cha) as a dessert dim sum item, and is also enjoyed as a breakfast snack or festive treat. Nutritionally, a 100 g serving provides approximately 285 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 42.5 g carbohydrate with 18.5 g sugar. The sodium content of 180 mg per 100 g reflects the salted egg yolk component. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 70 (high), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following a low-GI diet. The cake contains gluten, eggs, and dairy (condensed milk) and is not suitable for vegans or those with these allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Cantonese Thousand Layer Cake exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake and egg-enriched steamed cake composites, which are consistent with the input enriched_json values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.9). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using refined wheat flour sponge cake entries. Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of an exact approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown and moisture data for Cantonese Thousand Layer Cake are not available in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of April 2026. Saturated fat (3.2 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (95 mg per 100 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake fat composition data adjusted for egg yolk content. The input dietary_tag 'low_sodium' has been removed as 180 mg sodium per 100 g does not meet standard low-sodium thresholds (typically ≤120 mg per 100 g). The duplicate portion entry in the input has been deduplicated to a single 100 g serving. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate; a direct measurement study on this specific preparation is recommended for higher confidence. All core macros (protein_g 6.5, fat_g 10.5, carbohydrate_g 42.5) are non-zero and consistent with the steamed egg-flour-sugar cake profile; no zero-macro justification is required.
